events.title: WHO declares COVID-19 a global pandemic
events.description: The World Health Organization officially declared COVID-19 a pandemic, triggering global market sell-offs.

events.title: US announces COVID-19 lockdown guidelines
events.description: White House issues 15-day guidelines to slow the spread, effectively beginning US lockdowns.

events.title: Fed emergency rate cut to near zero
events.description: Federal Reserve cuts federal funds rate by 100bp to 0-0.25% in emergency action.

events.title: Fed raises rates 25bp — first hike since 2018
events.description: FOMC raises the federal funds rate by 25bp to 0.25-0.50%, beginning the post-COVID tightening cycle.

events.title: Fed raises rates 75bp — largest hike since 1994
events.description: FOMC raises by 75bp to 1.50-1.75% as inflation hits 8.6%.

events.title: Fed raises rates another 75bp
events.description: Second consecutive 75bp hike to 2.25-2.50%.

events.title: Russia invades Ukraine
events.description: Russia launches full-scale military invasion of Ukraine, triggering energy and commodity price spikes.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapses
events.description: SVB fails after a bank run triggered by unrealized losses on long-duration bonds. FDIC takes receivership.

events.title: Signature Bank closed by regulators
events.description: New York regulators close Signature Bank amid contagion fears from SVB collapse.

events.title: US presidential election — Biden wins
events.description: Joe Biden defeats Donald Trump in the 2020 presidential election.

events.title: US presidential election — Trump wins
events.description: Donald Trump wins the 2024 presidential election.

events.title: UK votes to leave the EU (Brexit)
events.description: The United Kingdom votes 52-48% to leave the European Union in a referendum.

events.title: US-China trade war — first tariffs take effect
events.description: US imposes 25% tariffs on $34B of Chinese goods; China retaliates immediately.

events.title: Fed signals rate cuts in 2024 (dovish pivot)
events.description: FOMC holds rates at 5.25-5.50% but dot plot signals 75bp of cuts in 2024.

events.title: GameStop short squeeze peaks
events.description: GME surges 135% in a single day as retail traders squeeze short sellers. Brokers restrict buying.

events.title: Evergrande officially defaults
events.description: Fitch declares China Evergrande Group in restricted default after missed bond payments.

events.title: BOJ widens yield curve control band
events.description: Bank of Japan surprises markets by widening 10-year yield target band from +/-0.25% to +/-0.50%.

events.title: UK mini-budget triggers gilt crisis
events.description: Chancellor Kwarteng announces unfunded tax cuts, crashing the pound and triggering BOE emergency bond buying.

events.title: FTX files for bankruptcy
events.description: FTX, the second-largest crypto exchange, files Chapter 11 after a liquidity crisis and alleged fraud.

events.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT
events.description: OpenAI releases ChatGPT, reaching 100M users in 2 months and sparking the generative AI investment boom.

events.title: US debt ceiling deal signed into law
events.description: Biden signs the Fiscal Responsibility Act, suspending the debt ceiling until January 2025.

events.title: Ever Given blocks the Suez Canal
events.description: Container ship Ever Given runs aground, blocking the Suez Canal for 6 days and disrupting global trade.

events.title: Noto earthquake strikes Japan (M7.6)
events.description: A magnitude 7.6 earthquake hits the Noto Peninsula in Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan.

events.title: NVIDIA Q4 earnings smash expectations
events.description: NVIDIA reports Q4 revenue of $22.1B (vs $20.4B expected), driven by AI chip demand.

events.title: Trump announces sweeping reciprocal tariffs
events.description: President Trump announces broad reciprocal tariffs on imports from multiple countries, sparking trade war fears.
